title: Angkor Ticket Sales Dip 34 Percent in January - Cambodianess
highlights: – Angkor Pass ticket sales for the Angkor Archaeological Park generated more than $4. 5 million in the first month of 2026, down 34 percent on the same period in 2025. A total of 93,491 tourists bought tickets in January, a 36 percent decrease, according to an Angkor Enterprise report. During the same month, 2,161 international visitors visited the Koh Ker archaeological site in Preah Vihear province,  ...    Out of the total revenue, Angkor Enterprise donated more than $1.5 million to the Kantha Bopha Foundation, which operates free children’s hospitals across the country. Established in 2016, Angkor Enterprise manages Angkor ticket sales, with the Ministry of Economy and Finance overseeing financial operations and the Ministry of Tourism responsible for technical matters. In 2025, Cambodia welcomed 5.5 million international visitors, down nearly 17 percent from 2024. However, tourism revenue reached an estimated $3.7 billion, up three percent year on the year. title: Gov't to Take Full Control of Angkor Wat, Sokimex Pushed Out
highlights: The government will take full control of the iconic Angkor Wat temple complex from Sokimex Group, which had been  ...    Sokimex received a concession to manage the temple complex in April 1999. The conglomerate, which runs fuel stations and hotels, is owned by tycoon Sok Kong. The controversial concession allowed his company to collect fees from ticket  ...    The government will form a new public enterprise to manage Angkor Wat that will comprise officials from the Ministry of  ...    Mr. Vandy said business people welcomed the move. “We want transparent management and for tourist to be treated  ...    visit the World Heritage Site,” he added. A report from the Apsara Authority, the government agency that manages the site, showed revenue from ticket sales at Angkor Wat rose 0.62 percent in the first 10 months of the year, to slightly more than $47 million. Ticket sales last month generated about $4.3 million in revenue, up 4.52 percent over the same month last year, the report said.

title: Angkor Wat Authority Defends Reported Ticket Revenues
